daniel-jaiyong-an-v-archblock-inc-2025-0.lesson: Courts now require GenAI-use certifications on future filings; build disclosure language into every brief template today.

dehghani-v-castro-2025-04-02.lesson: Outsourced drafting is still your signature; verify every cite from freelance work before filing or face dual-state bar referrals.

dangelo-v-vaught-2025-04-02.lesson: Vendor-branded legal AI tools like Smokeball's Archie still hallucinate; treat every output as unverified until cite-checked.

boggess-v-chamness-2025-04-01.lesson: Pro-se hallucinations get arguments ignored without sanctions; reserve energy for attorney-authored filings in similar disputes.

mckeown-v-paycom-payroll-llc-2025-03-31.lesson: Repeat pro-se hallucinations get pleadings struck but no fee-shift; move for strike early to clear the docket.

sanders-v-united-states-2025-03-31.lesson: Federal Claims court tolerates first-time pro-se AI errors with a warning; flag fabricated cites in your 12(b)(1) brief to accelerate dismissal.

kruglyak-v-home-depot-usa-inc-2025-03-25.lesson: Candor and prompt admission avoided monetary sanctions here; if AI use surfaces, disclose immediately rather than deny.

francois-v-medina-2025-03-24.lesson: New York trial courts are still issuing warnings rather than fines; this leniency window is closing fast.

loyer-v-wayne-county-michigan-2025-03-21.lesson: Mandatory ethics seminars are now a default professional sanction; budget CLE time as a cost of any unverified AI brief.

buckner-v-hilton-global-2025-03-21.lesson: Courts reject recusal motions built on judicial hallucination findings; preserve the issue for appeal instead.

mark-lillard-v-offit-kurman-pa-2025-03-1.lesson: Delaware Superior Court is now requiring forward-looking AI certifications from pro-se litigants; expect the same for attorneys soon.

arnaoudoff-v-tivity-health-incorporated-.lesson: Opponents benefit from flagging fake cites early; a motion to disregard was granted without needing full Rule 11 briefing.

nguyen-v-wheeler-2025-03-03.lesson: Apologizing and admitting AI "may have contributed" doesn't avoid Rule 11 fines; verify every cite on Westlaw or Lexis before filing.

bunce-v-visual-technology-innovations-1-.lesson: Novelty of AI is explicitly rejected as a defense; assume every ChatGPT cite is fabricated until independently verified.

merz-v-kalama-2025-02-25.lesson: AI-generated procedural advice is unreliable; Rule 15 leave-to-amend cannot be bypassed by a scheduling order.

wadsworth-v-walmart-morgan-morgan-2025-0.lesson: Internal firm AI platforms do not shield signers; every attorney on the signature block owes an independent Rule 11 inquiry.
